name: Calyptophilidae
description: Calyptophilidae is a small family of passerine birds endemic to the island of Hispaniola. These chat-like tanagers are secretive inhabitants of montane forests and are known for their distinctive vocalizations.

children.name: Calyptophilus
children.description: A genus of two species of thrush-like tanagers endemic to Hispaniola.

children.name: Calyptophilus frugivorus
children.description: The Eastern Chat-tanager is a large, olive-brown bird with a yellow throat and breast. It is endemic to lowland and montane forests in the Dominican Republic and is threatened by habitat loss.

children.name: Calyptophilus tertius
children.description: The Western Chat-tanager has olive-brown upperparts and a bright yellow throat and breast. It is found in montane forests of the Massif de la Hotte in Haiti and the Sierra de Baoruco in the Dominican Republic.
